title: Carrying my own cup to the cafe
summary: A small daily habit that quietly reduces plastic waste.
topic: Carrying my own cup to the cafe
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small daily or weekly habit you have started that is better for the environment. Be concrete, not preachy. Use present simple and present perfect.
hintStarters: Every morning before I leave my flat, I put a…
I have done this for about…
It is a small thing, but I noticed…
sampleText: Every morning before I leave my flat in Seoul, I put a small reusable cup into my bag with my keys and my phone. The cup is dark green, has a silicone lid, and fits perfectly in my hand. I have done this for about eight months now. When I order coffee at the cafe near the metro station, I just give the cup to the barista at the same time as I pay. She rinses it once with hot water, smiles, and fills it. The first few times, I felt a little awkward, because most customers were holding plastic take-away cups. After a week, the barista remembered my cup and started to charge me three hundred won less. That small discount surprised me more than I expected. I rinse my cup again at the office sink and dry it with a paper towel. It takes one extra minute in the morning. I do not throw away one plastic cup every day, which means more than two hundred cups since I started. I do not talk about it with my friends. It is too small a thing to feel proud of. But each morning, when I drink, I taste the coffee and the quiet feeling of doing one thing right.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một chiếc cốc nhỏ có thể tái sử dụng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I have done this for about eight months
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đã làm điều này khoảng tám tháng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: give the cup to the barista
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đưa cốc cho nhân viên pha cà phê
những cốc nhựa dùng một lần

sampleAnnotations.phrase: charge me three hundred won less
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tính rẻ hơn ba trăm won

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I do not throw away one plastic cup every day
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi không vứt đi một cái cốc nhựa mỗi ngày

sampleAnnotations.phrase: too small a thing to feel proud of
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: quá nhỏ để mà thấy tự hào

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the quiet feeling of doing one thing right
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cái cảm giác bình lặng khi làm đúng một việc

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect 'for + duration'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Have/has + V3 + for + khoảng thời gian' diễn tả hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và còn tiếp diễn: 'I have done this for about eight months.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Have/has + past participle + for + duration' shows an action started in the past and still ongoing: 'I have done this for about eight months.'

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for routines
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng present simple để mô tả thói quen hàng ngày: 'I put a small reusable cup into my bag', 'I rinse my cup again at the office sink.' Đi kèm trạng từ thời gian như 'every morning'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present simple for daily routines: 'I put a small reusable cup into my bag', 'I rinse my cup again at the office sink.' Often paired with time adverbs like 'every morning'.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng trước khi rời căn hộ ở Seoul, tôi đặt một chiếc cốc nhỏ có thể tái sử dụng vào túi cùng với chìa khóa và điện thoại. Cái cốc màu xanh đậm, có nắp silicon, và vừa khít trong tay tôi. Tôi đã làm điều này khoảng tám tháng nay. Khi tôi gọi cà phê ở quán gần ga tàu điện ngầm, tôi chỉ đưa cốc cho nhân viên pha cà phê cùng lúc với việc trả tiền. Cô ấy tráng cốc một lần với nước nóng, mỉm cười, và đổ đầy. Vài lần đầu tiên, tôi cảm thấy hơi ngại, vì hầu hết khách hàng đều cầm cốc nhựa dùng một lần. Sau một tuần, cô nhân viên đã nhớ chiếc cốc của tôi và bắt đầu tính rẻ hơn ba trăm won. Khoản giảm giá nhỏ đó làm tôi ngạc nhiên hơn tôi tưởng. Tôi tráng cốc lại ở bồn rửa văn phòng và lau khô bằng khăn giấy. Nó tốn thêm một phút mỗi sáng. Tôi không vứt đi một cái cốc nhựa mỗi ngày, nghĩa là hơn hai trăm cái kể từ khi tôi bắt đầu. Tôi không nói về điều đó với bạn bè. Nó quá nhỏ để mà thấy tự hào. Nhưng mỗi sáng, khi tôi uống, tôi nếm vị cà phê và cái cảm giác bình lặng khi làm đúng một việc.

options: I have done this for about eight months now.
I am doing this for about eight months now.
I do this since about eight months now.
I did this for about eight months now.
mcQuestions.explanation: Started in the past, still continuing → present perfect with 'for'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct present-simple routine sentence.
options: Every morning, I put a small reusable cup into my bag.
Every morning, I am putting a small reusable cup into my bag.
Every morning, I have put a small reusable cup into my bag.
Every morning, I will put a small reusable cup into my bag.
that can be used many times
that breaks easily
that is made of plastic
that is given as a gift

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'fits perfectly ___ my hand.'
options: in
on
at
by
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Fit in (something)' is the standard collocation for hands, bags, etc.

options: to wash something quickly with water
to fill something with hot coffee
to dry something with paper
to cover something with a lid
uncomfortable, not relaxed
very fast and confident
tired and hungry
loud and angry

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence with 'less' (comparing money).
options: She charged me three hundred won less.
She charged me three hundred won lesser.
She charged me less three hundred won.
She charged me three hundred less won.
more than two hundred cups
more than two hundreds cup
more than two hundreds cups
more than two hundred cup
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Hundred' stays singular after a number; the noun ('cups') is plural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence with 'too + adjective + to + verb'.
options: It is too small a thing to feel proud of.
It is too much small to feel proud of.
It is a too small thing for feel proud of.
It is so small for to feel proud of.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Too + adjective + a + noun + to + verb' is a slightly fancier but natural pattern.

options: a kind of plastic bottle
food or drink you take with you to consume elsewhere
an extra free dessert
a delivery service
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Take-away' = to-go, consumed away from the shop.

options: do a habit
make a habit
start a habit
drive a habit
mcQuestions.explanation: We 'start' (or 'develop') a habit naturally. 'Do/make/drive' a habit are not standard.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think small private habits (like carrying a reusable cup) really matter, or are big system-level changes the only thing that counts?
